title: APIC Cluster Health
dataSources: APIC cluster health API, APIC system logs/syslog
spl:
index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:system"
| search (cluster_status OR leader_election OR convergence)
| eval status=case(
    searchmatch("fully-fit"), "Healthy",
    searchmatch("partially-fit"), "Degraded",
    searchmatch("not-fit"), "Critical",
    1==1, "Unknown")
| stats latest(status) as cluster_status, latest(_time) as last_update by apic_id
| table apic_id, cluster_status, last_update
description: APIC controllers manage the entire ACI fabric. Cluster health issues (split-brain, leader election, convergence problems) can cause fabric-wide configuration and policy failures. Monitoring APIC cluster state is essential for fabric reliability.
value: APIC controllers manage the entire ACI fabric. Cluster health issues (split-brain, leader election, convergence problems) can cause fabric-wide configuration and policy failures. Monitoring APIC cluster state is essential for fabric reliability.
implementation: Monitor APIC cluster health endpoint every 30 seconds. Track cluster fitness, leader election events, and database sync status. Alert immediately on any non-fully-fit state. Monitor APIC resource utilization (disk, CPU, memory). Document recovery procedures for cluster issues.
visualization: Status grid (APIC cluster state), Timeline (cluster events), Single value (cluster fitness), Table (APIC node details).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `TA_cisco-ACI`, APIC system logs.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: APIC cluster health API, APIC system logs/syslog.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Monitor APIC cluster health endpoint every 30 seconds. Track cluster fitness, leader election events, and database sync status. Alert immediately on any non-fully-fit state. Monitor APIC resource utilization (disk, CPU, memory). Document recovery procedures for cluster issues.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:system"
| search (cluster_status OR leader_election OR convergence)
| eval status=case(
    searchmatch("fully-fit"), "Healthy",
    searchmatch("partially-fit"), "Degraded",
    searchmatch("not-fit"), "Critical",
    1==1, "Unknown")
| stats latest(status) as cluster_status, latest(_time) as last_update by apic_id
| table apic_id, cluster_status, last_update
```

Understanding this SPL

**APIC Cluster Health** — APIC controllers manage the entire ACI fabric. Cluster health issues (split-brain, leader election, convergence problems) can cause fabric-wide configuration and policy failures. Monitoring APIC cluster state is essential for fabric reliability.

Documented **Data sources**: APIC cluster health API, APIC system logs/syslog. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `TA_cisco-ACI`, APIC system logs. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cisco_aci; **sourcetype**: cisco:aci:system. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=cisco_aci, sourcetype="cisco:aci:system". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **status** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by apic_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Pipeline stage (see **APIC Cluster Health**): table apic_id, cluster_status, last_update


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with APIC health dashboards and per-object health; validate score against leaf/spine/tenant scope.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Status grid (APIC cluster state), Timeline (cluster events), Single value (cluster fitness), Table (APIC node details).
grandmaExplanation: APIC controllers manage the entire ACI fabric — so traffic problems, routing mistakes, and risky changes surface fast.
hardware: Cisco APIC, Nexus 9332C (ACI), Nexus 93180YC-FX (ACI), Nexus 9364C (ACI), Nexus 9504 (ACI), Nexus 9508 (ACI)
knownFalsePositives: Controller failovers during planned upgrades, scheduled HA tests, or after restart of management nodes.
